idea-plugin.name: AgentBridge
idea-plugin.description:
AgentBridge — ACP & MCP bridge connecting AI coding agents to your JetBrains IDE through 92 native MCP tools.

Instead of operating through a terminal or generating diffs in isolation, agents work through IntelliJ inspections, refactorings, the test runner, the build system, and Git — the same tools you use.

Multi-Agent Support

Connect your preferred AI coding agent and switch between them with one click. Supported agents:

  • GitHub Copilot — OAuth sign-in, dynamic model discovery
  • Claude CLI (Claude Code) — Anthropic's Claude via the claude CLI
  • Codex — OpenAI Codex via codex app-server, with device-auth sign-in
  • Kiro — Amazon Kiro agent
  • Junie — JetBrains Junie agent
  • opencode — open-source multi-model agent

Each agent has its own connection settings, tool permissions, and instructions.

Real IDE Integration, Not Just a Shell

Other coding agents can build, test, and use Git. AgentBridge does it through native JetBrains platform APIs — keeping you in the loop with visible, reviewable actions:

  • Inspections & quick-fixes — the agent sees the same warnings you see and can apply IDE-suggested fixes
  • Refactoring — rename, extract method, inline — through IntelliJ's refactoring engine, not text replacement
  • Test runner — executes tests in the IDE's Run panel so you can watch results live
  • Build tools — triggers incremental builds through IntelliJ, not a blind gradle build
  • Git operations — commits, diffs, and branches through the IDE's VCS layer with full editor buffer awareness
  • Code navigation — go-to-declaration, find references, type hierarchy, call hierarchy — using the IDE's semantic index
  • Static analysis — Qodana, SonarQube, and IntelliJ inspections available as agent tools

Every action is visible in the IDE. Nothing happens behind your back.

Human in the Loop

The agent works with you, not around you. You see every file change in the editor, every test result in the Run panel, every Git operation in the VCS log. You can pause, review, undo, or redirect at any point. Fine-grained per-tool permissions let you control exactly what the agent can do.

Works for Tasks of Any Size

  • Fix a failing test or a single inspection warning
  • Refactor across multiple files
  • Explore an unfamiliar codebase using IDE navigation
  • Implement a feature end-to-end with build and test verification

Privacy & Local-First Design

  • No telemetry or data collection
  • All operations executed locally inside your IDE
  • Your code never leaves your environment except through the connected agent

Requirements

  • A supported JetBrains IDE (IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, PyCharm, GoLand, etc.) version 2025.3+
  • An ACP-compatible agent CLI (GitHub Copilot, Claude CLI, Codex, Kiro, Junie, opencode, or custom)

Open Source

Apache 2.0 License — AgentBridge on GitHub

This plugin is an independent open-source project and is not affiliated with or endorsed by GitHub, Anthropic, Amazon, JetBrains, or OpenAI.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.16.0 — Codex, Nudge & PWA

OpenAI Codex Support

Full support for OpenAI Codex agents — drives the locally-installed codex CLI via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. Routes user input requests to the built-in ask_user flow; shows a window notification when a prompt arrives while the IDE is unfocused.

Nudge While the Agent Works

New Nudge action (Ctrl+Enter) lets you guide or redirect the agent mid-turn without stopping it. The nudge waits in a queue and is injected at the next opportunity. Cancel with the ✕ button if you change your mind.

Install the Chat as a PWA

  • Open the chat web UI in any browser and install it as a home-screen app
  • Download the certificate from /cert.crt and install it as a CA certificate once to trust HTTPS over your local network
  • Android: Settings → Security → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate → pick the downloaded file (do not just tap the file — that asks for a private key)
  • iOS/iPadOS: open the .pem file to install the profile, then enable it under Settings → General → About → Certificate Trust Settings
  • Certificate automatically includes your machine's LAN IP — no manual setup needed

1.15.0 — AgentBridge: New Agents & Rebranding

The plugin is now named AgentBridge.

New Agents

  • Claude CLI (Claude Code) — Anthropic's Claude via the claude CLI
  • Kiro — Amazon Kiro agent with thinking support
  • Junie — JetBrains Junie agent
  • Codex — OpenAI Codex via codex app-server; Sign In button runs codex login --device-auth in the IDE terminal

New MCP Tools

  • get_available_actions / apply_action / get_action_options — invoke any IDE quick-fix or intention action by name
  • get_sonar_rule_description — look up SonarQube rule explanations

Per-Agent Settings

Each agent has its own settings page with binary auto-detection, model selection, and a dedicated Agent Instructions section.

1.12.0

Multi-Agent Support

AgentBridge is no longer limited to a single agent. Configure and switch between multiple agents:

  • Agent profile selector in the connection panel — switch agents with one click
  • Per-profile settings: connection command, tool permissions, built-in tool blocking, custom instructions
  • Built-in profiles for GitHub Copilot and opencode, plus fully custom profiles
  • Agent and sub-agent names shown in chat bubble headers
  • Profile-specific message coloring in chat

Comprehensive Settings UI

Settings reorganized into structured sections:

  • MCP — Server configuration and individual tool enable/disable
  • ACP — Agent settings, profiles, and tool permissions
  • Other — Scratch file types, project files, and billing data
  • Plugin version and build info shown in the settings page footer

New MCP Tools (92 total)

  • undo / redo — undo and redo file changes
  • edit_text — surgical find-and-replace within a file
  • rename_file / move_file — file renaming and moving via IntelliJ VFS
  • find_implementations — find all implementations of a class/interface or method overrides
  • get_call_hierarchy — find all callers of a method
  • get_file_history — git history for a specific file (including renames)
  • read_build_output — read from the Build tool window
  • list_terminals / read_terminal_output — terminal tab management
  • list_project_files now supports sorting, size filters, and date filters

Permission System

  • Three-way permission prompt: Deny / Allow / Allow for Session
  • MCP tool annotations for granular permission control
  • Sub-agent built-in write tools automatically blocked via permission denial
  • Agent-level permission injection per profile

Follow Agent Mode Improvements

  • Status bar feedback during search operations
  • build_project no longer steals editor focus when Follow Agent is off

MCP Instructions

Plugin instructions are now sent via the MCP initialize response, with a fallback to copilot-instructions.md for agents that don't support it.

Code Quality

  • Gradle compile abuse detection — prevents runaway compilation tasks
  • Extensive SonarQube finding fixes across the codebase
  • Reduced cognitive complexity in core tool handlers

UI Cleanup

  • Removed debug, timeline, and help panels — cleaner tool window
  • Version number shown on the root connection panel and settings page

Bug Fixes

  • Profile names no longer gain "(Copy)" suffix on every settings open
  • MCP tools now work during modal dialogs
  • Fixed paste-to-scratch file bugs
  • Fixed git_reset path+commit handling
  • Fixed read_run_output for Gradle consoles and JUnit runners
  • Fixed backtick escaping in chat messages

1.5.0

Follow Agent Mode

The IDE now visually follows the agent as it works, enabled by default:

  • Highlight code as the agent reads/edits it
  • Editor shows "Agent is reading/editing" inlay labels
  • Project explorer marks files with read/write indicators
  • Git Log opens and selects commits after the agent commits

Redesigned Chat UI

Chat now looks and feels native to JetBrains:

  • JetBrains-style markdown — tables, accent-bordered code blocks, heading hierarchy, blockquote support
  • Quick-reply buttons — clickable suggestions with semantic colors and dismiss support
  • Code block enhancements — language labels, "Open in Scratch" button, word wrap toggle
  • Native Swing banners — status/auth/git banners use JetBrains InlineBanner

Scratch File Execution

Run code directly from chat — Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, Kotlin Script, Groovy, and Python scratch files.

Project Structure Management

New tool to manage module dependencies, libraries, and SDKs programmatically.

Conversation History

Search and recall past conversations across sessions.

Git Enhancements

  • 7 new git tools: fetch, pull, merge, rebase, cherry-pick, tag, reset
  • Commit hashes in chat are clickable links that navigate to the VCS Log
  • Git operations routed through IntelliJ Git4Idea infrastructure
  • Agent commits automatically appear in the Git Log

Theme and Terminal Tools

  • list_themes / set_theme — agent can switch your IDE theme
  • write_terminal_input — agent can interact with terminal sessions

Multi-IDE Compatibility

Java-specific code isolated so the plugin can run in WebStorm, PyCharm, and other non-Java IDEs.

Reliability

  • Auto-retry on stale Copilot sessions
  • Graceful handling of Copilot process crashes
  • Chat saved incrementally during streaming
  • Reduced rendering artifacts during streaming

1.0.0 — Initial Release

  • Introduces AgentBridge
  • Dynamic model discovery — automatically supports all Copilot models without plugin updates
  • Enables multi-step task execution inside the IDE
  • Project navigation and context awareness
  • Multi-file editing capabilities
  • Integration with inspections and refactorings
  • Test and build execution support
  • Git operation support
  • Local-first design with no telemetry

action.description: Send the current prompt text (or nudge if the agent is working)
Insert a new line in the chat prompt
Force-stop the current agent work and send the prompt
Queue the prompt to be sent after the agent finishes current work
